reasoning: The Lean formalization captures the informal statement accurately. The function `a : Finset.Icc 1 (2*n+1) → ℤ` represents 2n+1 integers indexed by the subtype. The hypothesis `h_remove` quantifies over all finsets S of size 2n in the 2n+1-element index type; each such S is the complement of a singleton (i.e., "removing one element"), so this is equivalent to the informal "if any one is removed". The existence of T ⊆ S with |T|=n and equal sums, combined with |S|=2n forcing |S\T|=n, precisely encodes "divided into two sets of n integers with equal sums". The conclusion ∀ i j, a i = a j matches $a_1 = \cdots = a_{2n+1}$. The edge case n=0 is vacuously handled consistently in both forms. No hypotheses are added or dropped.
